video_title: Guy Fieri Returns for *Dynamite* Thai Food in Arizona 🧨 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: The Wild Tiger
restaurants.guy_intro: And after that pick-up in 2014, Bo and I took a wild ride meeting a chef who's still firing up her untamed take on Thai. This is The Wild Tiger, a new version of Thai food. That's Chef Alishan Weaver, who's got the tiger by the tail with her own fierce take on this traditional cuisine.

dishes.description: Shrimp, pork, and crab dumplings, steamed first then deep-fried, served with dragon egg sauce and jalapeno rings.
dishes.guy_response: Not too spicy, not too salty. Dynamite. Shrimp, crab, chicken, pork, oyster sauce, a little bit of sugar. That dish has just skyrocketed.

dishes.description: Alaskan salmon battered in flour, pancake mix, and cornstarch, coated in seasoned panko breadcrumbs, deep-fried, and served with a tangy sweet sauce and sautéed vegetables.
dishes.guy_response: The salmon's fantastic. I haven't had tempura or fried salmon in the sauces and know that you scratch make all this. It's beautiful dish. All your dishes have such vibrant color. Ginger fish. The salmon melts in your mouth. You can get hints of the ginger. While you're eating it. It was such a decadent flavor. I loved it.

dishes.description: Pork marinated in coconut milk, Gold Mountain sauce, and spices, then grilled on skewers until caramelized.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. The coconut. Really, really good. A little caramelization of the sugar. Sugar. Mm. Ridiculous. You nailed it.

dishes.description: Seasoned panko breaded cocoa chili shrimp, deep-fried, and served with a coconut chili pineapple curry, steamed broccoli, spring onions, and fresh pineapple.
dishes.guy_response: Tender shrimp. Massive shrimp. Yep, yep. Delicious. Pineapple prawn. The prawns were spectacular. Who doesn't like deep fried shrimp? But then you have that punch of the pineapple. Seals the deal.
